page: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is an x86_64 architecture virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 4 physical AMD EPYC cores with 2 threads per core, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes a 32 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16, and SHA extensions. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 480 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all scoring in the top tier. However, due to the shared CPU allocation, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as seen in the stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU benchmarks. Memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations also perform within the average range. For machine learning workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small 135M parameter models, but exhibits weak performance during prompt processing on medium 7B models.

This server offers a balanced resource density of memory and NVMe storage relative to its shared CPU architecture, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. The primary tradeoff is the potential for resource contention on the shared AMD EPYC processor, which limits sustained multi-core execution. The hardware configuration is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, web serving, database operations, and lightweight text generation tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is an x86_64 virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on 4 physical cores with hyper-threading, the processor operates at 2.0 GHz with a 32 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained multi-threaded workloads but provides a cost-efficient resource profile for memory-intensive applications. This server is suitable for general-purpose web serving, database hosting, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g-480s is an x86_64 server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 480 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance alongside average multi-core execution.

tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors, balanced memory density, and local NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.

480 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Best fit for general-purpose web serving, database hosting, and light inference
